    Cascadia of Boise

    Part of Cascadia Healthcare

    • Skilled Nursing

    Cascadia of Boise

    6000 W Denton St

    Boise, ID 83704 Map

    (208) 629-4383

    cascadiaofboise.com

    Community size: Large

    Capacity
    99 residents

    About Cascadia of Boise

    Cascadia of Boise is a 99-bed Medicare- and Medicaid-certified skilled nursing facility specializing in ventilator and tracheostomy care and rehabilitation. Families should ask directly about nurse staffing levels per shift.

    Care offered here

    This profile isn't yet managed by the community, so it can't describe their programs in their own words. Below is a general description of each type of care offered at this community - for the specifics of how they do it, ask on your tour.

    • Skilled Nursing - what skilled nursing includes and who it fits

    Care needs usually grow over time. Ask every community what happens when your parent needs more care than their current level - moving twice is hard on everyone. See levels of care explained.

    Lifestyle & services

    These are the details we collect directly from each community: pets, couples, and age policies; medical support (nursing coverage, medication management, on-site therapies); non-medical services (housekeeping, meals, transportation); and how they handle concerns. Cascadia of Boise hasn't provided them yet, so ask a staff member when you call or tour. If you work here, an administrator can add them to this profile - here's how to manage this listing.

    Ratings & reviews

    We don't show a star rating ourselves - find it directly at the source. Start with the official record below, and it helps to know how to read an inspection record and judge how a community responds to complaints and violations.

    • Medicare Care Compare - federal record & rating
    • Search for more reviews - Google

    Full ratings & records section ↓

    What we can't confirm here

    Being straight with you about the gaps:

    • This profile hasn't been claimed by the community, so the details come from public sources, not from them directly.
    • Current pricing isn't listed - get a written quote at your parent's assessed care level.
    • Current availability and any wait list aren't confirmed.
    • Staffing levels, overnight nurse coverage, and turnover aren't independently verified - ask directly.
    • Services and care levels can change; confirm what your parent needs is offered now.

    Unknown is more honest than guessed. Confirm anything that matters on your tour.

    Do you work at Cascadia of Boise?

    Families make one of their biggest decisions with pages like this one - and nobody can describe your community better than the people who work there. If you're on staff or manage this community, you can update these details, answer short topic-by-topic questionnaires (each takes just a few minutes), and share photos of your community. It's free, and accurate listings help the right families find you.

    We know how demanding this work is, and this page isn't here to grade you - it's here to connect you with the right families. One thing families consistently want to know: how a community handles concerns when they come up. Our questionnaire gives you room to describe your process in your own words.

    Claim & update this listing

    Ratings & official records

    We don't publish a star rating or numeric score for Cascadia of Boise ourselves - ratings change, and a wrong number here would be worse than none. Instead, here's exactly where to find the real ones, straight from the source - and how to read an inspection record and judge how a community responds to complaints and violations.

    Whatever community you're considering, read the official record and recent family reviews below carefully and in full before you decide. We don't summarize or interpret any community's record ourselves - what matters is what you find at the source, today, and records are incomplete snapshots that can change, be disputed, or be resolved after we look. If anything you read concerns you, talk it through with the long-term care ombudsman - free, independent, and familiar with local communities - and with the community itself: how they respond tells you a lot.

    Medicare Five-Star Quality Rating

    Who assigns it: the federal Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS), the government agency that certifies nursing homes. What it measures: independent state health inspections, nurse and aide staffing levels, and clinical quality measures, combined into one overall star - reported separately too, since a facility can be strong on one and weak on another. The components draw on different data sources with different limits, so read the component ratings and the actual findings rather than the single overall star.

    See Cascadia of Boise's current rating on Medicare Care Compare →

    CMS certification number 135146. CMS refreshes ratings monthly - always check the live number on Care Compare rather than relying on any cached figure, including ones we might have shown in the past. How these stars are calculated, and their limits

    Family & resident reviews

    Who writes it: families and residents, on public review sites. What it reflects: day-to-day life - food, friendliness, communication - not inspections.

    • Search for more reviews of Cascadia of Boise →

    Whatever star average you find on those sites is a starting point, not a verdict. We don't write these reviews, verify what they say, or investigate the community ourselves - that's exactly why we send you to the source instead of showing you a number. Before you weigh what you find, know what it can hide:

    • Who writes them. Usually adult children, not the resident - so reviews capture communication, billing, and first impressions more than the texture of daily care. More →
    • Emotion moves the score. People post when they're delighted or furious; the quietly satisfied middle rarely does, and reviews written in grief, guilt, or relief carry that charge. The calm three-star reviews usually hold the real trade-offs. More →
    • Reviews can be solicited or self-reported. A community can fairly ask happy families to post - or cross the line by gating out unhappy ones, pressuring residents, or paying. A burst of five-star reviews in a single week is a flag, not a triumph. More →
    • Counts and dates matter. A 5.0 from three reviews is a coin flip, and new management makes a 2019 review irrelevant. Small homes gather few reviews by nature - lean on the state record and your own visit. Small homes →
    • Government stars are a different thing. Medicare's nursing-home stars aren't reviews at all - they come from inspections, staffing, and clinical data. How those work →

    Read the full guide to reading reviews without being misled →

    We link you to primary sources instead of showing a rating ourselves - why, and how to use what you find.

    This profile is a research aid, not a recommendation or a substitute for a professional assessment. Details verified against public sources on 2026-07-14. Sources: [1] [2] [3]. Services, staffing, prices, availability, ownership, and license status change - verify important details directly with the community and the responsible government agency before you decide. Community-provided statements are labeled and are not independently audited unless stated. See something inaccurate? Report it - or if you work at Cascadia of Boise, claim and manage this profile.

    Senior Living Community is an independent information resource for families, and the resident's quality of life comes first here - then the family, then the facility. We are not a referral service and do not provide medical, legal, or financial advice - for decisions about care, consult the professionals involved. Facility details change; always confirm directly with a community. Listings are never paid placements. Last data review: 2026-07-11. Run a senior living community? Claim your free listing or see our marketing services for communities. Terms · Privacy.
